page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c9ae.4xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing an AMD EPYC 9T95 processor running at a base clock speed of 2.25 GHz. With a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, each vCPU maps directly to a dedicated physical core, eliminating resource contention from hyperthreading.

The instance is provisioned with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached volumes. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 10 Gbps, and the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a resource density perspective, the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio positions this instance for compute-intensive workloads that require dedicated CPU execution without massive memory footprints. This hardware profile is appropriate for tasks such as batch processing, web serving, and distributed compute nodes where predictable CPU performance is prioritized over local storage IOPS or high memory capacity.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c9ae.4xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with 16 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9T95 processor operating at 2.25 GHz. Because the instance features a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, each vCPU corresponds to a dedicated physical core, ensuring consistent execution. The hardware profile includes 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB per core, with no local storage or GPU accelerators bundled. Network performance is anchored by a 10 Gbps baseline bandwidth. This resource configuration is designed for compute-heavy workloads such as batch processing, web serving, and dedicated application servers that require predictable CPU allocation without the overhead of hyperthreading or the need for high memory-to-CPU ratios.
